relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: After rupture, YHWH still speaks of the promised journey.
A messenger will go before them into contested land.
But presence in the midst is in crisis.
The people mourn and remove ornament.
The ornaments come down while YHWH considers the way forward.
Israel strips away adornment from Horeb onward.
Moses pitches the meeting tent outside camp.
The people watch Moses go out to seek YHWH.
Cloud descends at the tent opening, and YHWH speaks with Moses.
The people see cloud and worship from their tent doors.
YHWH speaks with Moses as companion to companion, while Joshua remains near.
Moses asks who will go with him and leans on being known by name.
He asks to know YHWH’s ways and remembers: this nation is your people.
YHWH answers: my presence/face will go, and I will give rest.
Moses refuses movement without Presence.
Only YHWH going with them distinguishes the people.
YHWH grants the request because Moses is known by name.
Moses asks: show me your glory.
YHWH answers with goodness, Name, grace, and compassion.
The full face remains beyond mortal seeing.
A place near YHWH is given on the rock.
Moses is sheltered in the cleft as glory passes.
He sees the afterglow, not the face.

contemplative_note: Exodus 33 is the Presence crisis: Moses refuses promise without Presence and learns that glory is revealed as goodness, Name, grace, and compassion.
